notes: Extended evaluation dataset adding entries C028-C031 for real-world pain points like vague memory, anchor selection, and broad term frequency.

entries.expected_target_description: Description of the specific artifact or conversation expected for this search.
entries.notes: THIS IS A PLACEHOLDER ENTRY ONLY. DO NOT USE FOR EVALUATION.

entries.expected_target_description: The AI Treasure Hunt Dev conversation containing the Captain's Code cipher puzzle design within the pirate treasure hunt game.
entries.notes: Direct keyword match test. Captain's Code is a distinctive named element that should resolve to the AI Treasure Hunt Dev conversation.

entries.expected_target_description: The AI Treasure Hunt Dev conversation where the Smuggler's Mark puzzle (symbol-to-location matching) was designed as part of the cutlass path branch.
entries.notes: Tests retrieval of a unique named game element combined with a mechanic description. Content also partially present in Target Audience Selection and Treasure Hunt System Design.

entries.query_text: where do players find fragment F1
entries.expected_target_description: The AI Treasure Hunt Dev conversation detailing that map Fragment F1 is found in the bedroom during the first step of the treasure hunt.
entries.notes: Semantic understanding test. Query asks 'where' without naming the bedroom. Fragment F1 content exists only in AI Treasure Hunt Dev.

entries.expected_target_description: The conversation about dinner inspiration and meal planning, distinct from all treasure hunt content.
entries.notes: Corpus discrimination test. Can the search isolate the one non-treasure-hunt conversation from a corpus dominated by treasure hunt content?

entries.query_text: map fragment distribution across game steps
entries.expected_target_description: Conversations discussing how the 4 map fragments (F1-F4) are distributed across game steps, including the structural fix that moved from branch-yields-fragments to branch-yields-clues. Primary source is AI Treasure Hunt Dev.
entries.notes: Thematic recall across multiple chunks. Fragment distribution is discussed primarily in AI Treasure Hunt Dev with related content in Pirate Pack Build Plan.

entries.query_text: fixing dead-end locations and broken game flow
entries.expected_target_description: Conversations where structural flaws in the treasure hunt (dead-end bedroom, broken branch fragment count, convergence error) were identified and resolved. Primary source is AI Treasure Hunt Dev, with some content in Target Audience Selection.
entries.notes: Semantic recall test. Uses paraphrases ('dead-end', 'broken game flow'). Content confirmed in AI Treasure Hunt Dev and Target Audience Selection.

entries.query_text: where did we decide that branch paths should give clues not fragments
entries.expected_target_description: The specific point in the AI Treasure Hunt Dev conversation where the decision was made that branch paths yield information/clues only, not map fragments.
entries.notes: Navigation to a specific design decision. Content 'branch gives clues not fragments' exists only in AI Treasure Hunt Dev.

entries.query_text: when did we introduce the location sheets concept
entries.expected_target_description: The point in AI Treasure Hunt Dev where the Location Sheets concept (visible environmental anchors representing map landmarks like volcano, jungle, lagoon, rocks) was introduced.
entries.notes: Navigation to a specific design introduction. 'Location sheets' content exists in AI Treasure Hunt Dev and Pirate Pack Build Plan.

entries.query_text: all discussions about treasure hunt planning and preparation
entries.expected_target_description: All conversations related to treasure hunt planning. Should surface AI Treasure Hunt Dev, Treasure Hunt System Design, AI Treasure Hunt Prep, AI Party Game Plan, Top German Treasure Hunt Sites, and Pirate Pack Build Plan.
entries.notes: Broad collection test. Should pull from multiple treasure-hunt-related conversations in the 33-conversation corpus. Tests recall breadth.

entries.query_text: everything about puzzles ciphers and physical clue hiding spots
entries.expected_target_description: All conversations containing puzzle design (Captain's Code, Smuggler's Mark), cipher mechanics, and physical clue placement (bedroom, dining area, shoes, curtains). Primary sources are AI Treasure Hunt Dev and Target Audience Selection.
entries.notes: Semantic breadth test with multiple related terms. Should surface AI Treasure Hunt Dev, Target Audience Selection, and AI Party Game Plan.

entries.query_text: what do we know about the target audience and age group for this product
entries.expected_target_description: All conversations touching on target audience, age group, difficulty tuning, and product positioning for the treasure hunt.
entries.notes: Synthesis-prep query requiring the system to gather scattered audience/demographic references across multiple conversations.

entries.query_text: rhyming pirate prose and narrative tone decisions
entries.expected_target_description: Conversations covering the rhyming pirate verse style, prose quality feedback, and narrative tone decisions for the treasure hunt script.
entries.notes: Tests ability to find creative/narrative content rather than structural/mechanical content. Semantically specific but not an exact phrase match.

entries.expected_target_description: Exact title match. The query is the literal title of a conversation. With phrase-matching boost, this should rank at position 1.
entries.notes: Step 448 EXACT PHRASE test. The query is identical to a conversation title. Tests that the phrase bonus in title field_score pushes this to rank 1.

entries.expected_target_description: Exact title match for Target Audience Selection conversation. With phrase-matching boost on title, this should rank at position 1.
entries.notes: Step 448 EXACT PHRASE test. Literal title query. Should benefit from the strong phrase bonus in title scoring.

entries.expected_target_description: Near-exact title match for Treasure Hunt System Design conversation. All four terms appear in the title, and the full phrase matches.
entries.notes: Step 448 COVERAGE + PHRASE test. All query terms present in the title field. Tests that full-term coverage bonus and phrase bonus together push the correct conversation to rank 1.

entries.expected_target_description: Single-word query. Should return AI Treasure Hunt Dev or other treasure-related conversations via pure semantic similarity, unaffected by multi-word boosts.
entries.notes: Step 448 SINGLE-WORD STABILITY test. Multi-word phrase/coverage/proximity bonuses should have zero effect on single-word queries. Confirms no regression from the new logic.

entries.expected_target_description: Exact title match for the Hyperliquid Perps Tutorial conversation. Completely unrelated to treasure hunt content. Tests that phrase matching correctly isolates non-treasure-hunt content.
entries.notes: Step 448 STRUCTURED UPLIFT test. This conversation exists in the corpus. The exact phrase in the title should strongly boost it to rank 1, even though it is semantically distant from the dominant treasure hunt corpus.

entries.expected_target_description: A multi-term query with all four terms scattered across the AI Treasure Hunt Dev conversation. Tests that high term-coverage in one chunk boosts it above results with only partial term overlap.
entries.notes: Step 448 COVERAGE test. Four distinct terms that individually appear in many conversations but all co-occur specifically in AI Treasure Hunt Dev chunks. Tests the coverage bonus for full-term hits.

entries.expected_target_description: Tight proximity query. All four terms appear close together in AI Treasure Hunt Dev where Location Sheets are described with their landmark names (volcano, jungle, lagoon, rocks).
entries.notes: Step 448 PROXIMITY test. These terms co-occur within a small region of the AI Treasure Hunt Dev text. Tests that the proximity bonus favours chunks where all terms appear together over chunks where they are scattered.

entries.query_text: how should we approach the overall game experience
entries.expected_target_description: Deliberately vague/ambiguous query with no exact phrase match in any conversation. Should surface multiple treasure-hunt planning conversations via semantic similarity without being distorted by lexical boosts.
entries.notes: Step 448 AMBIGUITY GUARD test. No conversation title or chunk contains this exact phrase. Confirms that lexical uplift does not overpower semantic search for genuinely ambiguous queries. Should still find relevant treasure-hunt-related conversations.

entries.expected_target_description: Exact title match for the AI Party Game Plan conversation. Tests whether title relevance correctly outranks conversations with higher body-term frequency for the same keywords.
entries.notes: TITLE-VS-BODY CONFLICT test. Keywords 'AI', 'Game', 'Plan' are extremely frequent in the massive AI Treasure Hunt Dev file, but this query is the literal title of a smaller document. Title weighting should ensure the specific plan ranks first.

entries.expected_target_description: Targeted search for the Grab Dinner Inspiration conversation. Tests whether a specific intent can overcome the broad frequency burial risk from the dominant treasure hunt corpus.
entries.notes: BROAD-FREQUENCY BURIAL test. Semantic and lexical match for a niche topic ('dinner') should prevail even when the corpus is dominated by another high-frequency topic ('treasure hunt').

entries.expected_target_description: Query combining two distinct game elements discussed in different parts of the AI Treasure Hunt Dev conversation. Tests whether multi-cluster relevance rewards the combined footprint.
entries.notes: MULTI-CLUSTER RELEVANCE test. 'Bedroom' and 'Smuggler's Mark' are separate clusters within AI Treasure Hunt Dev. The system should reward the document for containing both distinct matching regions.

entries.expected_target_description: Broad topical query about puzzle design. Tests whether a conversation with high topical density (many relevant chunks) outranks one with a single isolated strong match.
entries.notes: CONVERSATION-LEVEL TOPICALITY test. AI Treasure Hunt Dev has massive density of 'puzzle design' content. This should win over a document that has one locally high-scoring paragraph about puzzles.

entries.expected_target_description: General concepts around hosting an outdoor pirate party.
entries.notes: ADVERSARIAL: Mode 1/2 (Distributed vs Isolated). AI Party Game Plan is holistically about hosting the party (many distributed matches), but the massive AI Treasure Hunt Dev might contain one isolated dense chunk about an 'outdoor pirate party' and incorrectly win rank 1 under a max-chunk model.

entries.query_text: that one time we talked about fixing the dead-end branch
entries.expected_target_description: Recall the discussion where the dead-end branch flaw was fixed.
entries.notes: ADVERSARIAL: Mode 3 (Memory-style wording). Users often query using preamble words ('that one time we talked about'). A literal max-chunk ranker might find those exact common words in a completely unrelated document, outscoring the actual semantic target.

entries.query_text: printing physical maps and cutting map fragments
entries.expected_target_description: Instructions for printing and assembling physical materials.
entries.notes: ADVERSARIAL: Mode 4 (Large-file partial match bloat). The Pirate Pack Build Plan is definitively about this topic, but the massive 'AI Treasure Hunt Dev' file might string together enough hits for 'printing', 'maps', and 'fragments' in a single dense paragraph to steal rank 1.

entries.query_text: can we make the pirate clues rhyme like poetry
entries.expected_target_description: Discussion about pirate verse and rhyming clues.
entries.notes: VAGUE-MEMORY test. Simulates 'Pirate Poetry' vague recall. Should succeed via semantic match without an exact phrase.

entries.query_text: the fix where branch paths yield clues instead of map fragments
entries.expected_target_description: The particular excerpt where the branch path structural flaw was resolved.
entries.notes: ANCHOR-SELECTION test. This exact phrase occurs deep in the file. Will verify that the UI opens this optimal deep excerpt instead of the chronologically first excerpt.

entries.expected_target_description: Target Audience Selection conversation
entries.notes: TITLE-VS-BODY CONFLICT test. Lexical match in title should elevate this document despite its low relative term frequency compared to the main corpus.

entries.query_text: combining the captain's code cipher with the bedroom fragment
entries.expected_target_description: Query combining distinct game elements.
entries.notes: MULTI-CLUSTER test. Validates combo of elements that exist inside the same conversation but far apart.
